_note: Maps numeric claims in memo.md, memo.pdf, and blog_post_draft.md to verifiable sources. Delta A headline uses paired preference alignment (7/47 vs 43/47), not raw all-checks-pass (11/47).

training_artifact_paths._colab_note: Notebook wrote to training_artifacts/ in Colab; files copied to training/ in this repo.

claims.claim: bench_overcommitment triggered on 100% of bench-feasibility probes in Week 10 (40/40)
Inter-rater agreement 0.91 overall; format_check 0.87 (24-hour compliant pass, 30 tasks, 64 check-level comparisons)
Training set: 91 preference pairs, 80 unique chosen bodies, 6 failure categories, 0 contamination hits (8-gram train vs held-out input check on 91 pairs)
claims.verification_note: Pair and category counts reproducible via wc -l and jq; unique chosen via dedup of chosen field; violation_count in contamination JSON.

claims.claim: SimPO selected over DPO: no reference model, fits T4 at workable batch size
Training wall time ~2.16 minutes on Tesla T4; training billed cost $0 (Colab)
Train-split preference accuracy after 3 epochs: 100%, mean margin ~0.7515
Descriptive: Week 10 candidate outputs pass all deterministic checks on 11/47 = 23.4% held-out tasks (not the Delta A metric)
Delta A baseline: deterministic preference alignment 7/47 = 14.9% (evaluator prefers ground_truth over candidate or tie)
Delta A trained: LoRA judge preference accuracy 43/47 = 91.5%
Delta A lift +76.6pp (14.9% → 91.5%), 95% paired bootstrap CI [+63.8pp, +87.2pp], exact paired binomial/McNemar p = 0.000000000015
Inference latency p50 ~368.76 ms (trained LoRA judge, held-out preference pass)
Inference latency p50 ~96.06 ms (prompt-only same backbone, held-out preference pass)
Delta B: +42.6pp (48.9% → 91.5%), 95% paired bootstrap CI [+25.5pp, +59.6pp], exact paired binomial/McNemar p = 0.000017940998
Contamination: 91 preference-pair training rows checked, 0 violations (training_data/contamination_preference_pairs.json)
